rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN B. NELSON VS. BRENDAN M MCGRATH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 02, 2020 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JONATHAN NELSON MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN B. NELSON VS. BRENDAN M MCGRATH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 02, 2020 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JONATHAN NELSON DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to December 23, 2020. Plaintiff to provide a supplemental brief addressing ONLY (1) citations to cases addressing former owners/persons formerly in possession claims for recovery for trespass and encroachment; no argument to be included; and (2) whether new owners are necessary parties to this action; this portion of the supplemental brief shall be limited to 2 pages. Defendant to provide a supplemental brief addressing ONLY whether new owners are necessary parties to this action; supplemental brief shall be limited to 2 pages. Supplemental briefs to be filed no later than December 18, 2020. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK W MAHAN VS. JAMES ASHLEY FERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 02, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT JAMES FERRY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED with final leave to amend. The Court ordered Plaintiff to allege facts in its ruling on the Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff added several conclusory allegations and no facts. Defendant's Motion to Strike Second Amended Complaint is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) showing that the reimbursement for expenses and labor that went into the listing and selling of the property is covered by 1983 written partnership agreement (attaching the agreement and pointing to relevant provisions may be one way to make the proper allegations) and that transfer of $40,000 was a profit sharing by the partners (facts regarding the terms of the profit sharing related to $40,000 payment in exchange for deeds and how this transfer is based on/I furtherance of the partnership agreement) unrelated to a discrete transaction re: portions of front and back yards. All allegations shall be made in good faith and address the sham pleading issue. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. GILBERTO CORDOVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 02, 2020 line 6 . DEFENDANT GILBERTO CORDOVA MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is GRANTED. Default is set aside based on mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). While it is unclear how counsel calculated the due date for the response without consulting a calendar, which would have revealed that October 29, 2020 was a Thursday, such miscalculation is a valid basis for relief. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JIU XIANG NI VS. CYNTHIA THOMPSON
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 02, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT CYNTHIA THOMPSON DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. No opposition filed. The Court was informed that Plaintiff submitted a letter to the Court. This letter has not been reviewed as it constitutes an improper ex-parte communication. =(501/REQ)
